text.title: Infrastructure-as-Code Lint Floor (shellcheck, hadolint, yamllint, actionlint, tflint) Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Every IaC file format MUST have its dedicated linter run: shellcheck (.sh), hadolint (Dockerfile), yamllint (*.yml), actionlint (.github/workflows/*.yml), tflint (.tf).
rule.rationale: Format-specific linters catch class-specific bugs (e.g. hadolint catches Dockerfile-specific issues yamllint misses).

rule.statement: IaC linters run at the SAME pre-commit + CI tier as code linters. Not optional / deferred.
rule.rationale: IaC bugs reach production as deploy failures; same gate as code keeps blast radius bounded.

rule.statement: .shellcheckrc / .hadolint.yaml / .yamllint / .tflint.hcl MUST be committed with explicit rule set.
rule.rationale: Default rules may be too permissive or too strict; committed config makes rules visible.

rule.statement: Excluded rules MUST carry a justification comment in the rc file.
rule.rationale: Silent exclusions accumulate; named ones are reviewable.

rule.statement: CI MUST fail the PR on any IaC lint violation. Warnings escalate to errors.
rule.rationale: Warnings get ignored; only failure changes behaviour.

rule.statement: If repo is pure-Python/Go with no IaC artefacts OR team has TF Cloud / Atlantis covering Terraform OR scripts are trivial throwaway, STOP per relevant file.
rule.rationale: Forcing IaC lint where there's no IaC is overhead.
